description_long: This traffic barricade consists of three horizontal planks supported by two vertical posts, all constructed from what appears to be metal. The front-facing side of the planks is marked with alternating black and yellow diagonal stripes, a common pattern used for high-visibility warning in urban environments. The rear side of the barricade is plain yellow, lacking any striping or signage. The support legs are wide and flat, providing stability and preventing tipping. The surface of the barricade shows some wear and weathering, suggesting it is designed for outdoor use. There are no visible lights, signage, or attachment fixtures on the structure.
description: A metal traffic barricade with three horizontal planks, black and yellow diagonal stripes on the front, and sturdy flat support legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the barricade and the flat, wide support legs.
description_view_1: Front view displaying three horizontal planks with bold black and yellow diagonal stripes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the thin profile and support legs.
description_view_3: Rear view showing three plain yellow planks without any striping or signage.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Three horizontal planks with alternating black and yellow diagonal stripes, stripes are of equal width and repeat across the front face.
